text.title: GraphQL Schema Design Patterns
text.summary: Type system conventions, interfaces, error union patterns, and SDL naming rules for schema-first GraphQL APIs.

rule.statement: Always design the SDL before writing resolver code. The SDL diff is the artifact a human reviews — never let an agent autopatch a resolver without showing the schema change first.
rule.rationale: Schema-first aligns frontend/backend teams on a stable contract before implementation. Resolver code is derived from the schema, not the other way around.

rule.statement: Use interfaces (Node, Timestamped) to share common fields. Implement PascalCase for types, camelCase for fields, SCREAMING_SNAKE for enums. Pin in schema linter.
rule.rationale: Consistent naming across a large schema makes SDL readable and allows schema linters to enforce conventions automatically.

rule.statement: Mutations return a typed payload with a union of success type and typed error types. Use union types (Result) for expected errors; leave errors[] for unexpected failures.
rule.rationale: Union types make expected failure modes explicit in the schema and force clients to handle them. Untyped errors[] signals a bug, not a business rule.

rule.statement: All mutations take a single input argument of an input type. Never use individual scalar arguments for mutations.
rule.rationale: Input types can be extended with optional fields without breaking existing callers. Individual scalar arguments cannot be extended non-breakingly.

text.reference: Full SDL skeleton with Node/Connection/Payload patterns
